examples.title: Winged-Edge Data Structure Definition
examples.description: Demonstrates how winged-edge structure stores mesh connectivity
examples.code:
STRUCT Edge
  Vertex v1, v2          // Two endpoint vertices
  Face f1, f2            // Adjacent faces on each side
  Edge e1, e2, e3, e4    // Adjacent edges (winged structure)
                        // e1,e2 on f1 side; e3,e4 on f2 side
END STRUCT

STRUCT Vertex
  Point3D position       // x, y, z coordinates
  Edge edge              // One incident edge
  List<Face> faces       // All faces containing this vertex
END STRUCT

STRUCT Face
  Edge edge              // One edge bounding the face
  List<Vertex> vertices // Vertices of this face
END STRUCT

FUNCTION getFaceVertices(Edge e, Face f)
  // Traverse around face f starting from edge e
  currentEdge = e
  REPEAT
    vertex = currentEdge.v1  // Get starting vertex
    vertices.add(vertex)
    currentEdge = currentEdge.getNextEdgeAroundFace(f)
  UNTIL currentEdge = e
  RETURN vertices
examples.explanation: Winged-edge stores each edge with pointers to its two vertices, two adjacent faces, and four neighboring edges. This enables O(1) traversal from any edge to its adjacent elements.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(E) where E is number of edges

examples.title: Half-Edge Data Structure Implementation
examples.description: Python implementation of half-edge for mesh face traversal
examples.explanation: Each edge is represented by two half-edges. Following 'next' pointers around a face traverses the boundary. Following 'opposite' accesses the adjacent face across an edge.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) to traverse all edges around a face where n = face degree
examples.spaceComplexity: O(E) for half-edges + O(V) for vertices + O(F) for faces

examples.title: Catmull-Clark Subdivision Algorithm
examples.description: Demonstrates one iteration of Catmull-Clark subdivision on quad mesh
examples.explanation: Catmull-Clark creates smooth surfaces from polygonal meshes. Face points and edge points are computed, then original vertices are repositioned using weighted average of neighboring points. After first iteration, all faces become quads.
examples.timeComplexity: O(E + V + F) per subdivision iteration
examples.spaceComplexity: O(V' + E' + F') where new counts are roughly 4x original

examples.title: Mesh Adjacency List Operations
examples.description: C implementation of adjacency list for mesh vertex neighbors
examples.explanation: Adjacency list stores for each vertex a list of its neighboring vertices. This enables efficient finding of all adjacent vertices but requires searching to check if two vertices are connected.
examples.timeComplexity: O(k) where k = vertex valence for neighbor lookup, O(V²) for building
examples.spaceComplexity: O(V + E) for storing adjacency lists
